purchase_note: You do not need an expensive branded kit to get started. Choose an option that suits your budget and make sure the listing says Zigbee rather than Wi-Fi or Bluetooth. Search links are neutral, have no affiliate tags, and availability varies by country and seller.
zigbee_note: For most common sensors and smart plugs, a Zigbee or Zigbee 3.0 label is enough: Zigbee2MQTT discovers the device and it then appears in GrowerHub. Check the exact model ID in the Zigbee2MQTT catalogue when possible to see available metrics and commands, but there is no need to buy only expensive brands.
wifi_note: Most retail Wi-Fi sensors from Tuya and similar ecosystems depend on the manufacturer's cloud and do not let you configure GrowerHub MQTT. Suitable exceptions are devices with open local MQTT configuration and GrowerHub hardware.

coordinators.title: Zigbee coordinators for GrowerHub
coordinators.description: Proven USB coordinators for Zigbee2MQTT: affordable CC2652P sticks and SONOFF options.
coordinators.intro: A particular brand is not required: affordable CC2652P USB sticks and other adapters from the Zigbee2MQTT list work well with GrowerHub. SONOFF remains a common branded option.

items.name: Budget coordinator with external antenna
items.summary: A practical and affordable CC2652P option for Zigbee2MQTT. Similar sticks are available from many sellers.
items.image_alt: Example appearance of a Zigbee USB coordinator with an external antenna
items.image_caption: Example appearance: the enclosure and antenna vary by seller.
notes: In Zigbee2MQTT the zstack adapter is selected
Take the option with coordinator firmware
An external antenna is convenient for a greenhouse and several rooms

items.name: USB coordinator on Z-Stack
items.summary: Common coordinator on CC2652P. Suitable for those who prefer a device from a well-known brand.
items.image_caption: Model image from the open Zigbee2MQTT documentation.
notes: In Zigbee2MQTT the zstack adapter is selected
A USB extension cable helps remove the coordinator from radio interference

items.name: USB coordinator on Ember
items.summary: Current coordinator at Silicon Labs for those who choose the SONOFF line.
items.image_caption: Model image from the open Zigbee2MQTT documentation.
notes: In Zigbee2MQTT the ember adapter is selected
Version E is similar in appearance to ZBDongle-P - see markings

sensors.title: Zigbee sensors for getting started
sensors.description: Accessible Zigbee temperature, air humidity and soil sensors for your first GrowerHub connection.
sensors.intro: For the first monitoring, one Zigbee temperature and humidity sensor is sufficient. The screen is convenient for checking readings on site, and soil and leakage sensors can be added at any time.

items.name: Temperature and Humidity with LCD Screen
items.summary: An accessible sensor that shows values locally and sends them to Zigbee2MQTT.
items.image_alt: Example appearance of a Zigbee temperature and humidity sensor with a display
items.image_caption: Example appearance: generic models use different enclosures and model IDs.
notes: The screen makes it easy to compare local readings with the dashboard
In the product card, select the option Zigbee 3.0
The actual model ID GrowerHub will show after connection

items.name: Soil moisture, temperature and humidity
items.summary: One Zigbee sensor provides several indicators for a pot, bed or greenhouse zone at once and does not require a separate cloud application.
items.image_alt: Example appearance of a combined Zigbee soil sensor
items.image_caption: Example appearance: available metrics depend on the exact revision.
notes: Convenient as the next step after the air sensor
The set of indicators depends on the specific revision
For automatic watering, first compare the readings with a manual soil test

items.name: Temperature and humidity
items.summary: Compact battery sensor for basic climate monitoring.
items.image_caption: Model image from the open Zigbee2MQTT documentation.
notes: Model support is described in Zigbee2MQTT
The compact housing is convenient to place inside the area

items.name: Temperature and humidity with screen
items.summary: Shows values locally and passes them to Zigbee2MQTT.
items.image_caption: Model image from the open Zigbee2MQTT documentation.
notes: The screen is convenient for on-site inspection
Model support is described in Zigbee2MQTT

items.name: Temperature, humidity and pressure
items.summary: The compact Aqara sensor transmits temperature, humidity and atmospheric pressure.
items.image_caption: Model image from the open Zigbee2MQTT documentation.
notes: Model support is described in Zigbee2MQTT
Good option for compact placement

items.name: Water leakage sensor
items.summary: Helps you notice water on the floor near the tank, pump, or sprinkler connections.
items.image_caption: Model image from the open Zigbee2MQTT documentation.
notes: Reports leak status and battery charge
Place at the lowest point of possible leakage

sockets.title: Zigbee smart plugs for control
sockets.description: Accessible Zigbee smart plugs for lighting, ventilation and other equipment in GrowerHub.
sockets.intro: For basic control, a regular Zigbee smart plug rated for the connected load is enough. Affordable white-label models can work just as well as branded options, and GrowerHub shows the available commands after connection.

items.name: Affordable smart plug with timer
items.summary: This affordable Zigbee smart plug works reliably through Zigbee2MQTT and is suitable for manual control and schedules.
items.image_alt: Example appearance of a 16 A Zigbee smart plug
items.image_caption: Example appearance: white-label models use different enclosures.
notes: Tested in a working GrowerHub installation
In the product card, select the Zigbee version
For light and other loads, observe the rated power

items.name: Zigbee smart plug
items.image_caption: Model image from the open Zigbee2MQTT documentation.
notes: Check the plug and socket standard for your country
Do not exceed the rated load

items.name: Zigbee smart plug with monitoring
items.summary: Power management and consumption metrics available in Zigbee2MQTT.
items.image_caption: Model image from the open Zigbee2MQTT documentation.
notes: Choose the TPF version for European sockets
Metrics do not replace electrical protection

items.name: Zigbee smart plug with energy monitoring
items.summary: The Aqara smart plug reports state, power, voltage, current and accumulated energy use.
items.image_caption: Model image from the open Zigbee2MQTT documentation.
notes: Support for commands and metrics is described in Zigbee2MQTT
Select the load according to the socket data sheet

pump.title: GrowerHub irrigation pump — early access
pump.description: Our Zigbee and Wi-Fi MQTT pump is being tested with early GrowerHub users.
pump.summary: We developed the pump because we could not find a ready-made option with the local control we needed. We are testing Zigbee and direct Wi-Fi MQTT operation before inviting the first users.
limitations: We check physical isolation, protection from water and safe power connection.
We add a local emergency shutdown regardless of the command from the platform.
We check the safe state when the Internet or MQTT is lost.
We test TLS, failure scenarios and watering limits before connecting the first users.
